title: RPK Documentation Overrides
description: Schema for overriding auto-generated rpk command documentation. Provides comprehensive controls for technical writers to customize, exclude, deprecate, and enhance command documentation.

textTransformations.description: Global text transformation rules applied to all command descriptions during generation. Allows writers to define patterns that should be formatted as inline code or replaced consistently across all commands.

replacements.description: Text replacements applied BEFORE inline code formatting. Use for normalizing text (e.g., STDOUT → stdout). Applied in order.

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to match text that should be replaced. Use JavaScript regex syntax.

replacement.description: Replacement text. Can use $1, $2, etc. for capture groups, or $& for entire match.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this transformation does

flags.description: Regular expression flags. Default: 'g' (global). Common: 'gi' (global, case-insensitive)

inlineCode.description: Patterns to wrap in inline code (backticks). Applied AFTER replacements and AFTER code block protection. Use negative lookbehind/lookahead to avoid double-wrapping.

oneOf.description: Regular expression pattern. Matched text will be wrapped in backticks.

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to match text that should be wrapped in backticks

replacement.description: Optional custom replacement (e.g., '`$1`' for first capture group). If omitted, wraps entire match in backticks.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this pattern matches

definitions.description: Reusable definitions that can be referenced via $ref

commands.description: Command-specific overrides, keyed by full command path (e.g., 'rpk topic create')

description.description: Override the command description. Replaces only the main description; sections from rpk output are preserved.

appendToDescription.description: Content to append to the auto-generated description instead of replacing it.

introducedInVersion.description: Version when this command was introduced (e.g., 'v26.2.0')

$refs.description: Array of $ref paths to merge into this command (e.g., ['#/definitions/common-kafka-flags'])

exclude.description: If true, exclude this command from documentation entirely. Use for internal or hidden commands that shouldn't be documented.

excludeFlags.description: List of flag names to exclude from documentation (without dashes)

excludeExamples.description: List of regex patterns to exclude specific examples from the source EXAMPLES section. Matches against example descriptions or commands.

deprecated.description: Mark this command as deprecated. A deprecation notice will be shown.

deprecatedMessage.description: Custom deprecation message explaining what to use instead. Only used when deprecated is true.

deprecatedInVersion.description: Version when this command was deprecated (e.g., 'v26.1.0')

removedInVersion.description: Version when this command will be or was removed (e.g., 'v27.0.0')

replacement.description: For deprecated commands, specify what command or approach to use instead. This is displayed in the deprecation notice.

minVersion.description: Minimum rpk version required for this command (e.g., 'v25.3.0')

platforms.description: Platforms where this command is available. If not specified, assumed available on all platforms.

prerequisites.description: Prerequisites or requirements before using this command

seeAlso.description: Related commands or documentation pages. Can be strings (always shown) or objects with cloudOnly/selfHostedOnly properties for platform-specific links.

oneOf.description: xref link or command name (shown on all platforms)
Conditional see-also item shown only on specific platforms

content.description: The xref link or command name

cloudOnly.description: If true, only shown in cloud docs (wrapped with ifdef::env-cloud[])

selfHostedOnly.description: If true, only shown in self-hosted docs (wrapped with ifndef::env-cloud[])

pageAliases.description: AsciiDoc page aliases for redirects from old URLs (e.g., 'reference:rpk/rpk-cluster/rpk-cluster-metadata.adoc' or multiple aliases separated by commas)

excludeAliases.description: List of alias names to exclude from documentation (e.g., intentional misspellings for autocorrect)

aliasNotes.description: A note to display after the aliases section explaining non-obvious aliases (e.g., autocorrect aliases)

content.description: Unified content additions. Use this for all custom content (sections, cloud-only, self-hosted, admonitions, includes). Each item has: type, position, and type-specific fields.

pageAliases.description: Comma-separated list of page aliases for backwards compatibility. Preserves old URLs when commands are renamed. Example: 'reference:rpk/old-name.adoc, features:guide.adoc'

pageAttributes.description: Custom page attributes to add to the header (e.g., page-topic-type, learning-objective-*)

cloudOnly.description: If true, this command only appears in cloud documentation (entire page wrapped in ifdef::env-cloud[])

selfHostedOnly.description: If true, this command only appears in self-hosted documentation (entire page wrapped in ifndef::env-cloud[])

_note.description: Internal note for writers. Not used in documentation generation. Use for explaining override rationale or tracking issues.

flagOverrides.description: Flag-specific overrides, keyed by flag name (without dashes)

deprecatedMessage.description: Custom deprecation message for the flag

required.description: Override whether the flag is marked as required

validValues.description: List of valid values for this flag (shown in docs)

cloudOnly.description: Flag only available in cloud environment

selfHostedOnly.description: Flag only available in self-hosted environment

introducedInVersion.description: Version when this flag was introduced (e.g., 'v26.2.0'). Auto-populated by diff when new flags are detected.

contentItem.description: A content item to add to the documentation. The 'type' field determines what kind of content and which additional fields are required.

contentPosition.description: Where to insert this content in the document

contentItemSection.description: A custom section with optional title. Supports subsections and parent sections with dynamic heading levels. If 'id' matches a source section (e.g., 'EXAMPLES', 'FIELDS'), you can: (1) use 'exclude: true' to remove it, or (2) provide 'content' to replace its content.

id.description: Unique identifier for this section. If it matches a source section ID (e.g., 'EXAMPLES', 'FIELDS'), you can exclude or replace that section.

title.description: Section title (e.g., 'Examples', 'Concept'). Use null for content without a section header.

content.description: Section content in AsciiDoc format. Omit if using subsections.

subsections.description: Array of nested subsections. Template automatically adjusts heading levels (=== for subsections, ==== for sub-subsections, etc.)

parent.description: ID or lowercase title of an existing rpk section to nest this section inside. Template automatically adjusts heading levels. Example: 'flags' nests this section inside the Flags section.

exclude.description: If true, exclude a section from the rpk source output (use when overriding with custom content). The generator checks all case variations of the ID.

headingLevel.description: Override the automatic heading level (2 for ==, 3 for ===, etc.). Use sparingly - template usually determines this automatically.

contentItemCloudOnly.description: Content shown only in cloud docs (wrapped in ifdef::env-cloud[])

contentItemSelfHosted.description: Content shown only in self-hosted docs (wrapped in ifndef::env-cloud[])

contentItemNote.description: A NOTE admonition. Use plain text only - template automatically wraps in NOTE: or [NOTE]====...==== depending on complexity.

content.description: Note content. Plain text with inline code/xrefs. Template detects complexity (includes, multiple paragraphs) and uses block format automatically.

contentItemWarning.description: A WARNING admonition. Use plain text only - template automatically wraps in WARNING: or [WARNING]====...==== depending on complexity.

content.description: Warning content. Plain text with inline code/xrefs. Template detects complexity (includes, multiple paragraphs) and uses block format automatically.

contentItemTip.description: A TIP admonition. Use plain text only - template automatically wraps in TIP: or [TIP]====...==== depending on complexity.

content.description: Tip content. Plain text with inline code/xrefs. Template detects complexity (includes, multiple paragraphs) and uses block format automatically.

contentItemCaution.description: A CAUTION admonition. Use plain text only - template automatically wraps in CAUTION: or [CAUTION]====...==== depending on complexity.

content.description: Caution content. Plain text with inline code/xrefs. Template detects complexity (includes, multiple paragraphs) and uses block format automatically.

contentItemImportant.description: An IMPORTANT admonition. Use plain text only - template automatically wraps in IMPORTANT: or [IMPORTANT]====...==== depending on complexity.

content.description: Important content. Plain text with inline code/xrefs. Template detects complexity (includes, multiple paragraphs) and uses block format automatically.

contentItemInclude.description: An include directive to add a partial or shared content

contentItemExample.description: A single structured example. Writers provide plain text; template generates AsciiDoc code block automatically.

description.description: Optional description shown before the code block (plain text, inline code allowed)

code.description:
The code content. Template wraps in [,language]
----
...
----

contentItemExamples.description: Multiple structured examples with optional section title. Template generates proper AsciiDoc section with code blocks.

output.description: Optional expected output from running the command

subsection.description: A subsection within a parent section. Template automatically determines heading level based on nesting depth.

id.description: Optional ID for this subsection (used for anchors)

subsections.description: Nested subsections (can be nested arbitrarily deep)
